name: chart-bar
title: ChartBar
description: Single-series bar chart for comparing discrete categories or evenly spaced time buckets on a band-scaled axis.

intent.summary: Single-series bar chart for comparing discrete categories or evenly spaced time buckets on a band-scaled axis.
useWhen: Users need to compare one numeric measure across a small set of categories.
Values are grouped into ordered time buckets such as hours, days, weeks, or months and equal visual spacing is appropriate.
A data-viz card needs a compact magnitude comparison rather than a continuous trend.
avoidWhen: Timestamp positions must reflect irregular elapsed time; pre-bucket the data or use a future continuous time-scale chart.
The visualization needs stacked, grouped, or multi-series bars.
The interaction requires selection, drill-down, brushing, or zoom.
The data communicates a part-to-whole relationship.

alternatives.when: Users need to follow one or more trends across ordered categories.
alternatives.reason: ChartLine connects ordered observations and supports multiple named series.

alternatives.when: The data communicates parts of a total.
alternatives.reason: ChartDonut provides the part-to-whole composition and center aggregate.

accessibility: Keep category labels concise and provide an adjacent textual summary when exact values are critical to completing a task.
Do not rely on bar color alone to communicate category meaning.
A future interactive tooltip must supplement rather than replace the chart's accessible value representation.
states: The chart is currently a static single-series rendering without hover, selection, loading, or empty-state policy.
An empty dataset renders no bars; the owning card or product supplies meaningful empty-state messaging.
Use one consistent bar color across time buckets when every bar is the same measure at a different interval; categorical color changes are for genuinely distinct categories.
Authored datum colors override the shared categorical palette.
responsiveBehavior: The owner supplies dimensions and updates them with its layout; the chart does not measure its container.
Limit or pre-format long category labels so they remain legible at the chosen width.
Time labels remain ordered, pre-formatted buckets with equal spacing rather than raw continuous timestamps.

customElements: Assign data as a JavaScript property; arrays are not supported as HTML attributes.
react: Pass a stable data array and dimensions derived from the containing layout.
angular: Bind data as a property and update dimensions from the containing layout.

data.description: Bars to render. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).
Bars to render. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).

consumption.complexPropertyNote: Assign these non-primitive values as JavaScript properties: data.

peerDependencies.frameworks: Custom Elements; React 18/19 wrappers; Angular 19-22 standalone adapters.
